patches.hypothesis: Refined the patch by introducing a more robust EOF detection method, handling edge cases more comprehensively, and adding more test cases to cover all possible scenarios. The overfitting patterns are also addressed by avoiding hardcoded values and ensuring that the code is reusable using more generic solutions.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug was caused by the incorrect handling of the end-of-file (EOF) condition, which resulted in an infinite loop. The fix involves checking for EOF before reading from the input stream and returning -1 if EOF is encountered.

patches.hypothesis: The test is expecting an IOException to be thrown, but the method is not throwing any exception when the entry size is exceeded. The method should throw an IOException in this case.

The bug is caused by the incorrect use of the `available()` method, which can return 0 even if there are still bytes to read. The fix is to use a different method to check if there are bytes available.
patches.changes: Changed `numToRead = Math.min(numToRead, available());` to `numToRead = Math.min(numToRead, is.available());`
patches.diff: Changed `numToRead = Math.min(numToRead, available());` to `numToRead = Math.min(numToRead, is.available());`
